mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.14% is near zero, indicating an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates suppress long-term Treasury yields through the expectations channel: market participants anticipate continued monetary accommodation, reducing the term premium demanded on 10-year Treasuries. As FEDFUNDS remains anchored near zero, 10-year yields decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 416,633 million dollars and housing starts (HOUST) at 954 thousand units indicate moderate demand. When real sales growth decelerates below trend while housing construction remains flat, aggregate demand weakens, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors seek safe havens and repricing occurs downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND HOUST remains below 1000 thousand units
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite both RSAFS and HOUST meeting trigger conditions, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 7.9% is elevated relative to historical full employment levels. Persistent labor market slack suppresses wage growth expectations and inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as the market reprices inflation risk downward and reduces the real rate premium demanded.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points over the 60-day window while UNRATE remains above 7.5%, or the signal produces negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 98.62 (index 2017=100) is below trend, indicating supply-side weakness. Constrained supply combined with elevated unemployment suggests stagflationary pressures are absent; instead, deflationary forces dominate, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower as real yields become more attractive and inflation expectations compress.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points over 20 days when INDPRO < 99.0, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 230.979) combined with near-zero FEDFUNDS (0.14%) creates deeply negative real rates. Market participants expect eventual monetary tightening to restore positive real rates. This expectation of future rate increases causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market front-runs anticipated Fed action and reprices the term structure upward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite both conditions met, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 13,647.6 billion chained 2017 dollars) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS at 134,825 thousand) shows moderate economic activity. When employment growth stalls while GDP remains flat, the market interprets this as early-cycle weakness, reducing long-term growth expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors reprice terminal rate expectations downward.
regime_hint: early-cycle growth deceleration with labor market softening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over 60 days despite employment growth below 50k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 134,825 thousand) shows solid labor market participation. When employment growth accelerates above 200 thousand per month, it signals robust demand and inflation risk, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices inflation expectations upward and increases the term premium demanded for holding long-duration bonds.
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong job creation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 5 basis points over 5 trading days when monthly payroll growth exceeds 200k, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 98.62) combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE at 7.9%) indicates supply constraints without demand pressure. This regime produces stagflationary expectations where investors demand higher yields to compensate for inflation risk without corresponding growth. 10-year Treasury yields rise as the real yield floor increases and term premium expands.
regime_hint: stagflationary supply shock with labor market slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points over 20 days when both conditions are met, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
